title: peraxilla uniflora
intro: Peraxilla uniflora, also known as the Kaviano or Kawakawa, is a small, flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used for centuries in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The health benefits of Peraxilla uniflora include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Peraxilla uniflora is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and roots are used in various herbal preparations.
